mgmt.description: Infrastructure & control plane: hypervisors, backup, firewall, controllers, identity, cicd

srv._comment: Generic single-zone service network for simple deployments. Use srv-home/srv-work/srv-cust instead for SOHO multi-zone setup.
srv.description: Generic service zone — activate for simple single-zone deployments

srv-home._comment: Personal/household services (Home Assistant, personal stack). home/work reach this via their own access-to; mgmt via its access-to.
srv-home.description: Personal services: home automation, personal apps

srv-work._comment: Business/internal services. dmz pinhole = reverse-proxied public apps. work/home reach via their access-to.
srv-work.description: Business services: internal apps, AI/productivity stack

srv-cust.description: Customer/tenant sandboxes — mutually isolated, public via dmz only

srv-dev._comment: Development services zone — internal only, not public-facing. Set state=Active when development workloads are deployed.

home.description: Trusted personal/staff-personal devices: laptops, phones, tablets

work._comment: Managed/corporate workstations. Set state=Disabled for pure-home deployments.

iot._comment: Generic single-zone IoT network for simple deployments. Use iot-local/iot-cloud/iot-cams instead for SOHO multi-zone setup.
iot.description: Generic IoT zone — activate for simple single-zone deployments

iot-local._comment: Trusted local automation: NO internet, NO LAN. Zigbee/Hue/KNX/SAP/Shelly. Controller (HA in srv-home) reaches it via pinhole; mgmt via its access-to. (= The Hook Up's 'NoT'.)
iot-local.description: Local-only IoT: no internet, HA-controlled automation

iot-cloud._comment: Internet-required AND trusted (HA-controlled or in a dependency): Smart home appliances (e.g. Siemens, Bosch, Miele, etc), media (Chromecast/Sonos), solar/HVAC/BMS cloud monitoring. Sonos/SSDP also needs udpbroadcastrelay 1900 (see _README.mdns_policy).
iot-cloud.description: Internet-dependent trusted IoT: cloud-monitored utilities, media, voice

iot-cams._comment: Cameras + NVR. No internet, no LAN. Only NVR/viewer (run as a srv module) reaches feeds via pinhole. Replace srv-home with the zone hosting the NVR if different. Usually wired PoE (no SSID).

iot-untrust._comment: OPTIONAL — set state=Active when you own internet-required devices with NO trust relationship (random cheap gadgets, untrusted vendor/firmware). Internet only; no LAN; no inter-device; isolated from iot-cloud so a compromise cannot move laterally to trusted devices. See _README.iot_classification.split_policy_3_questions.
iot-untrust.description: Untrusted internet IoT — quarantine: internet only, fully isolated

guest.description: Visitors — internet only, full client isolation

dmz._comment: Reverse proxy + public-facing. access-to internet ONLY (Lars rule). Backends (srv-work/srv-cust) declare 'dmz' in their pinhole-allowed-from.

test1.description: Test zone 1 — Inactive by default; activated by firewall test.sh --deep to validate end-to-end inter-zone rules

test2.description: Test zone 2 — Inactive by default; activated by firewall test.sh --deep to validate end-to-end inter-zone rules

test3.description: Test zone 3 — Inactive by default; activated by firewall test.sh --deep. Deliberately has 'test1' only in pinhole-allowed-from and NOT in access-to so a consumer in test1 must rely on an auto-pinhole (issue #173) to reach a provider in test3.
